templates.use_when: Another manager brings you a failing employee
templates.body:
WRONG SEAT INTAKE · [Business]
From manager: _______________  Employee: _______________  Date: _______

Stated problem:
_____________________________________________

Their read (discipline vs bored):
_____________________________________________

My answer today: observe first · write-up later only if discipline signals
Observation window end: _______  Owner: _______

OBSERVATION LOG · Day ___ of ___
Employee: _______________

Task type / complexity: _______________________
Energy (flat / ok / lit up): ___________________
Speed vs peers on this task type: ______________
Unofficial help on hard stuff (Y/N + note): ____
Attendance / tardy note: ______________________

Mismatch signal today (Y/N): ___________________
Discipline signal today (Y/N): _________________

PRIVATE CONVERSATION (in person, not Slack pile-on)

"I sat with you this week. I do not think you are lazy. I think you are bored in a seat that is too simple for what you can do.

Examples I saw: [specific task vs spark task].

I want to try one hard assignment before any write-up. If it works, we move you. If it does not, we talk honestly about fit.

Are you willing to try?"

TEST ASSIGNMENT · one only
Employee: _______________  Due: _______________

Task (real work, not homework):
_____________________________________________

Done means:
_____________________________________________

Unblock contact: _______________  Observer notes due: _______________

Success criteria: quality + on-time + they ask for more hard work

CROSS-TEAM HANDOFF

To receiving manager: _______________
Employee: _______________

Why move: bored in [old seat], spark on [hard work type].
Test result: _______________
New seat start date: _______________
Success metrics for first 30 days: _______________

Old manager stops public criticism. New manager owns onboarding week 1.

TEAM NOTE (short, no diagnosis)

"[Name] is moving to [new queue/role] starting [date] to focus on [hard work type]. Route [work type] to them there. Questions to [manager]."

NEW SEAT METRICS · 30 days
Employee: _______________  New seat: _______________

Hard-work quality (1-5): ___
On-time rate: ___
Peer pull (asks for their help): Y/N
Owner would staff this seat again: Y/N

Adjust seat / celebrate / escalate: _______________

templates.use_when: Test failed but effort was visible
templates.body:
HONEST FIT CONVERSATION

"We tried the hard assignment. It did not land. That does not make you a bad person. It means this seat and the test seat are not the right match.

Options we owe you: [alternate seat if real] / [clean exit with reference focus on strengths] / [documented discipline path if signals were mixed].

What do you want to do next?"

DISCIPLINE PATH (mismatch ruled out)

Document: dates, policy, witnesses, prior coaching.

"This is not a boredom issue. The pattern is the same on easy and hard work. Here is what has to change by [date] or we part ways."

Next step: PIP / exit per local practice. Legal review if unsure.

30-DAY CHECK · Wrong Seat Rescue
Employee: _______________

Still lit up on hard work (Y/N): _______________
Regression to old seat habits (Y/N): _______________
Customer/peer feedback: _______________

Decision: keep / adjust metrics / manager coaching needed
